questions.text: Walk me through a project you've built — what did it do, and what was the hardest technical decision you made?
How would you explain what an algorithm is to someone who has never programmed?
If a program you wrote suddenly got 100 times more users, what would you check first and why?
Tell me about a bug that took you a long time to find. How did you eventually track it down?
Tell me about a time you worked on code with someone else. How did you divide the work?
Describe something you've designed or built with your hands or with software tools. What constraints did you have to work within?
If a design you were confident in failed during testing, how would you go about finding out why?
Talk me through the trade-offs you'd consider when choosing between a simple solution and a more robust one.
Tell me about a time a project didn't go as planned. What did you change?
Tell me about a biology topic or experiment that genuinely fascinated you. What made it interesting?
If you ran an experiment and got a result opposite to what you predicted, what would you do next?
How would you explain a scientific concept you know well to someone with no science background? Pick one and try it.
Describe a time you had to be very careful and precise in your work. How did you keep track of the details?
What experiences have you had with healthcare — as a volunteer, patient, or observer — that shaped how you think about medicine?
A patient disagrees with a treatment their doctor recommends. What considerations matter in that situation?
Tell me about an ethical dilemma — real or hypothetical — and how you would reason through it.
Describe a time you supported someone who was struggling. What did you do, and what did you learn?
Tell me about a product or company you find interesting. What do you think makes it work?
If you had to decide whether a new idea was worth pursuing, what information would you gather first?
Describe a time you had to persuade someone. What was your approach?
Tell me about a goal you set for yourself and how you tracked your progress toward it.
Tell me about a book, idea, or piece of work that changed how you think. What changed?
Pick an argument you've encountered that you disagree with. Can you steel-man it — present its strongest version — before explaining your view?
How do you decide whether a source or claim is trustworthy?
Describe a piece of writing or research you're proud of. What was your process?
What got you interested in this field in the first place?
Why this program in particular — what are you hoping to find here that you couldn't find elsewhere?
Where do you hope to be five years from now, and how does this step fit into that?
Tell me about a project or activity you've put real time into. What kept you coming back to it?
Describe a time something didn't go the way you wanted. What did you do afterward?
Tell me about something you taught yourself. How did you go about learning it?
What's something you've changed your mind about? What changed it?
Tell me about a time you worked with people who thought differently from you.
Outside of your main field, what do you spend your time on, and why?
Tell me about your experience with the relevant systems.
questions.note: Deliberately underspecified: 'systems' is ambiguous. Asking which systems are meant is an ideal response.

questions.text: How do you usually handle pressure?
questions.note: Deliberately broad: time pressure, social pressure, and workload are all valid readings. Asking which kind is meant is an ideal response.

questions.text: Walk me through how you would prepare the materials we discussed.
questions.note: Deliberately underspecified: no materials were discussed. Asking what materials are meant is an ideal response.
